title: monardella breweri subsp. glandulifera
intro: Monardella breweri subsp. glandulifera, also known as Brewer's monardella glandulifera, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, native to the southwestern United States. This plant is rich in volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Monardella breweri subsp. glandulifera include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and effectiveness.
